title: Spergularia capillacea
taxonomy: Spergularia capillacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Spergularia, which contains approximately 30 species of annual or perennial plants. The genus Spergularia is part of the order Caryophyllales, which includes plants with five-petaled flowers and a distinctive leaf shape. Spergularia capillacea is a member of the tribe Alsineae, a group of plants characterized by their small flowers and delicate appearance. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Caryophyllaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
